question_text: Explain why we cannot hear sound in space, even though the Sun is very loud.
correct_answer: Sound needs a medium (like air, water, or solid material) to travel through. Space is a vacuum with no particles, so sound waves cannot propagate.
explanation: Sound requires particles to vibrate and pass the vibrations along. In the vacuum of space, there are no particles to carry the sound waves, so sound cannot travel there at all.
